title: Passport a valid document for SIR identification, say Election Commission officials
cleaned_news: Election Commission of India (ECI) officials today said Indian passports continue to be among the 12 valid supporting documents that voters can use to establish eligibility for inclusion on the electoral rolls under the ongoing special intensive revision (SIR). Officials were responding to a query after a controversy erupted when the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) clarified that a passport is not proof of citizenship under the Passport Act of 1967. The response comes amid controversy sparked by the MEA's recent clarification that a passport is a travel document, not proof of citizenship, and should not be regarded as conclusive proof of citizenship.
bullets: • Election Commission of India (ECI) officials confirmed that Indian passports remain among the 12 valid documents for voter identification in electoral rolls.
• The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) clarified that a passport is not proof of citizenship under the Passport Act of 1967.
• An Indian passport is primarily a travel document and should not be considered conclusive proof of citizenship.
• Citizenship is governed by the Citizenship Act, while passports are issued under the Passports Act, highlighting different legal frameworks.
• Former Foreign Secretary Nirupama Menon Rao stated that a passport is powerful evidence of citizenship in everyday life but not the final legal authority in citizenship disputes.

vocabularies.meaning_en: serving to prove a case; decisive or convincing.
said again or repeated something already said, typically for emphasis or clarity.
(of a problem, conflict, or emotion) suddenly break out or intensify.
conducted the policy, actions, and affairs of (a state, organization, or people).
serving as a sign or indication of something.

error_spotting.sentence: Officials was responding to a query after a controversy erupted.
error_spotting.rule: Subject-verb agreement: The plural subject 'Officials' requires the plural verb 'were'.

fill_in_the_blanks.sentence: Indian citizenship can be established through documentation related to birth, descent, registration, or naturalization, but not through a passport _____, which is not considered final proof in legal disputes.

jumbled_parts: that you are an Indian citizen

title: US-Iran war news LIVE: Washington mulls relocating bases amid Gulf military's posture review, says report
cleaned_news: President Donald Trump said the United States plans to use Iranian assets frozen under US sanctions to purchase wheat, soybeans and corn from American farmers, while Iran's Parliamentary Speaker MB Ghalibaf called these claims false, stating Iran harvests mistrust. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Washington remains committed to negotiating an end to the conflict with Iran but will not accept any agreement that allows Tehran to impose fees on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, a stance backed by Oman during Gulf Cooperation Council talks, even as a vessel was struck by an unidentified projectile in the Strait, renewing concerns about safe passage. Meanwhile, the United States is reviewing its military deployment in the Middle East, considering shifting bases farther west, and US-mediated negotiations between Israel and Lebanon have been extended amidst a fragile ceasefire, with the UN atomic watchdog chief emphasizing a strong verification system for Iran's nuclear program.
bullets: • President Donald Trump announced the US plans to use frozen Iranian assets to purchase American wheat, soybeans, and corn.
• Iran-backed Hezbollah alleged a "flagrant" ceasefire breach by Israel, claiming a drone strike killed three civilians in southern Lebanon.
• US Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated Washington would not accept any agreement allowing Iran to impose fees on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz.
• A cargo vessel was struck by an unidentified projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, damaging its bridge and renewing concerns about safe passage.
• The United States is reviewing its military deployment in the Middle East, considering relocating bases farther west to avoid Iranian missiles and drones.

vocabularies.meaning_en: thinks about (a matter) deeply and at length.
(of an action) conspicuously or obviously offensive.
the meaning of a word, phrase, or text.
interrupt (an event, activity, or process) by causing a disturbance or problem.
(of a situation) uncertain or precarious; easily broken or damaged.

error_spotting.sentence: The United States is reviewing its military deployment in the Middle East, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing satellite images that suggests significantly greater damage at a major naval base in Bahrain.
error_spotting.rule: Subject-verb agreement. The relative pronoun "that" refers to "satellite images" (plural), so the verb must be plural.

fill_in_the_blanks.sentence: Yesterday, the United Nations _____ its evacuation of ships through the Strait of Hormuz after the British military reported that a vessel was struck by a projectile off the coast of Oman.

jumbled_parts: but will not accept any agreement
Washington remains committed to negotiating an end to the conflict with Iran
that allows Tehran to impose fees on ships

title: US Supreme Court clears Trump immigration moves on temporary protected status and asylum policy — Explained
cleaned_news: The US Supreme Court has handed the Trump administration two major immigration wins, allowing it to end protections for hundreds of thousands of migrants and potentially revive restrictive asylum policies at the US-Mexico border. The 6–3 rulings strengthen executive authority over immigration while sharply limiting judicial oversight, affecting Temporary Protected Status for migrants from Haiti and Syria and permitting revival of a controversial “metering” asylum policy.
bullets: • The US Supreme Court allowed the administration to end Temporary Protected Status (TPS) for migrants from Haiti and Syria.
• The rulings strengthen executive authority over immigration while sharply limiting judicial oversight.
• The Court permitted revival of a controversial “metering” asylum policy that limits how many migrants can apply at border crossings each day.
• Temporary Protected Status (TPS) is a humanitarian immigration program created by Congress in 1990, allowing people to stay temporarily if their home country is unsafe.
• The liberal justices dissented, arguing that courts do have authority to ensure proper legal procedure and that evidence suggests the TPS rollback may have been influenced by racial animus.

vocabularies.meaning_en: limiting freedom of action or choice.
hold or express opinions that are at variance with those previously, officially, or popularly expressed.
erode the base or foundation of; lessen the effectiveness, power, or ability of, especially gradually or insidiously.
an earlier event or action that is regarded as an example or guide to be considered in subsequent similar circumstances.

error_spotting.sentence: The 6–3 rulings strengthens executive authority over immigration while sharply limiting judicial oversight.
error_spotting.rule: Subject-verb agreement: A plural subject ('rulings') requires a plural verb ('strengthen').

fill_in_the_blanks.sentence: Temporary Protected Status (TPS) is a _____ immigration program created by Congress in 1990.

jumbled_parts: The US Supreme Court has handed the Trump administration
two major immigration wins, allowing it to end protections
for hundreds of thousands of migrants and potentially revive
restrictive asylum policies at the US-Mexico border.

title: No deal with US without tariff advantage framework: Piyush Goyal at UK-India Week 2026
cleaned_news: Union commerce minister Piyush Goyal said India will not sign the proposed bilateral trade agreement with the US until a framework guaranteeing a comparative tariff advantage for Indian exports is finalised. India and the US have reviewed progress on the interim trade pact, covering market access, digital trade and supply chains. Both countries are working to conclude an agreement before the July 24 deadline for the expiry of Washington's temporary 10 per cent tariff regime.
bullets: • Union commerce minister Piyush Goyal stated India will not sign the proposed bilateral trade agreement with the US.
• The agreement will not be signed until a framework guaranteeing a comparative tariff advantage for Indian exports is finalised.
• India and the US have reviewed progress on the interim trade pact.
• The interim trade pact covers market access, digital trade and supply chains.
• Both countries aim to conclude an agreement before the July 24 deadline for the expiry of Washington's temporary 10 per cent tariff regime.

error_spotting.sentence: Union commerce minister Piyush Goyal said India will not sign the proposed bilateral trade agreement with the US until a framework guaranteeing a comparative tariff advantage for Indian exports are finalised.
error_spotting.rule: Subject-verb agreement: The singular subject 'framework' requires the singular verb 'is'.

fill_in_the_blanks.sentence: Both countries are working to conclude an agreement before the July 24 deadline for the expiry of Washington's temporary 10 per cent tariff _____.

jumbled_parts: India and the US have reviewed progress
